playbook.intent: Cross-quarter migration completes with zero data loss, proven rollback, bounded dual-stack window, and clean post-migration audit.
playbook.scope: A multi-quarter migration (Postgres major upgrade, monolith-to-services decomposition, or equivalent) goes from "we should probably do this" to "post-migration audit clean." The team builds a pre-migration baseline, proves dual-write / shadow-read, executes a cutover from a tagged runbook, and runs the post-migration audit. Risk register is alive throughout. All squads are informed at each gate.

success-criteria.criterion: Dual-write + shadow-read evidence collected for ≥2 weeks
Cutover runbook executed end-to-end with sign-offs

stage.name: Strategy + scope
stage.intent: Current/future state + gap analysis turn intuition into a written change strategy.
stage.decision-gate: Advance when change-strategy is signed off and risk register is reviewed across all impacted squads.

stage.name: Architecture + boundaries
stage.intent: Service boundaries, quality attributes, and reliability/observability baselines defined.

tasks.task: Run quality-attributes analysis with reliability + observability focus
Pick patterns: ACL, circuit breaker, saga, inter-service comm

outputs.output: Decision log for patterns picked / rejected

stage.decision-gate: Advance when boundaries are testable in a spike and the rejected patterns are written down with rationale.

stage.name: Backups + reversibility
stage.intent: Proven backups + verified restore + rollback plan before any cutover.

tasks.task: Wire GitOps progressive delivery so reverts are cheap

stage.decision-gate: Refuse to advance without a proven restore inside the last 30 days.

stage.name: Dual-stack + shadow-read
stage.intent: Run old and new side-by-side; capture parity evidence.

tasks.task: Wire shadow-read on the new stack
Pin a golden-master for legacy rewrite checks

outputs.output: Parity report from ≥2 weeks of dual-stack

stage.decision-gate: Advance when parity is within target band on production traffic shape and open deltas have owners.

stage.name: Cutover
stage.intent: Tagged runbook executes end-to-end; comms loop closes.

tasks.task: Execute runbook as markdown tagged steps
Run comms-management cadence: pre, mid, post
Hold change-control gate at the critical step

stage.decision-gate: Required: every tagged step has a 'done' marker. Skipped steps = block-and-investigate.

stage.name: Post-audit + lessons
stage.intent: Audit verifies the new state; lessons close the loop.

tasks.task: Project closure with explicit benefits realisation

stage.decision-gate: Required outputs: audit + lessons-learned. Migrations without retros teach nothing to the next one.

extra.value: [Greenfield architecture — assumes an existing production system, Routine feature delivery — covered by rfc-to-production-feature-delivery, Vendor selection / licence procurement — handled by separate procurement track]

[Business case approved with explicit cost-of-doing-nothing, Backup + restore tested in last 90 days, 'Observability stack in place (metrics, logs, traces)', On-call rotation able to absorb cutover window]

[Skipping the restore drill — backups that don't restore aren't backups, Dual-stack with no parity report — flying blind, Cutover runbook in a wiki nobody reads — fails the bus-factor test, Comms only after the fact — surprises customers and on-call]

['Can I restore last night''s backup into a new cluster from cold steel?', 'Did parity hold across all critical paths under real traffic shape?', 'Do I have a written rollback that someone other than the migration lead can execute?']

{human: faion get-content big-migration-postgres-monolith --format md, agent: faion get-content big-migration-postgres-monolith --format context, json: faion get-content big-migration-postgres-monolith --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: Initial draft for F-064 P6 product-dev team persona}]
